explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The mintXFAN() function at line 307 has no access control on msg.sender — only a notBlacklisted(miner) check on the parameter address, not the caller. Any non-blacklisted external user can call mintXFAN(attacker_address, large_amount, DIRECT) and the contract will use its own USDT balance to fund a DEX swap (swapUsdtXFAN), minting XFAN tokens to the attacker without the caller depositing any USDT. The _contractMintROI address is declared but never used as a guard. A complete exploit chains mintXFAN (to receive XFAN) then unstakeXFAN (to convert XFAN to ETH), draining the contract's ETH.
